title: Papermerge
description: Self-hosted document management system (DMS) for digital archives: scan, OCR, organize, tag and full-text-search PDFs and scanned documents.
installation: Deploy with the bundled PostgreSQL and Valkey (Redis) add-ons; rotate the Postgres password and the random PAPERMERGE__SECURITY__SECRET_KEY placeholder (openssl rand -hex 32), and change the bootstrap superuser password PAPERMERGE__AUTH__PASSWORD before going to production. The web container listens on internal port 80 (apache) and serves both the React UI and the REST API. Uploaded documents and OCR output live under /db/media (PAPERMERGE__MAIN__MEDIA_ROOT) on the persistent volume. On first boot the database is migrated automatically and a superuser is created from PAPERMERGE__AUTH__USERNAME / PAPERMERGE__AUTH__PASSWORD; log in and change the password immediately. OCR is performed by separate companion worker images that must be deployed alongside this app and share the same Postgres, Redis and media volume: papermerge/ocrworker:0.3.1 (OCR), papermerge/i3worker:0.3 (full-text index writer), papermerge/path-tmpl-worker:0.4.1 (path templates). Full-text search defaults to a built-in SQLite/Tantivy index (PAPERMERGE__SEARCH__URL unset) so no external Elasticsearch is required for a single-node install; set PAPERMERGE__SEARCH__URL only if you front it with Solr/OpenSearch. If you switch document storage to S3 instead of the local volume, also deploy papermerge/s3worker and set PAPERMERGE__S3__BUCKET_NAME.
